There are twenty types of temples enumerated "here by me as
follows J (1) Meru, {2) Mandara, (3) Kailasa, (4 ■ Vimanacchanda,
(6) Nandana, (6) Samudga, (7) Padma, (B) Garuda, (9) Nandivar-
dhana, (10) Kufijara (11) Guharaja, (12) Vrsa, (13) Harhsa, (14)
Sarvatobhadraka, (15) Gha^a, (16) Simha, (17) Vrtta, (18)
Catuskona, (19) ^odasa^ri, and (20) Asta^ri. I shall now explain
their characteristics in regular orden
l^o||
Among them, the type of temple known as Meru is hexangular,
has twelve storeys and internal windows of various kinds. It has four
doors or gates in the four directions and is 32 cubits in width. (Its
height is 64 cubits).
